results.title: 10 Strategies for Using AI to Enhance Business Operations
results.content:
💡 Tip: Look for AI solutions that understand your business context, not just generic responses. Coworker changes this through OM1 (Organizational Memory) technology, building deep context across 120+ business parameters (projects, teams, priorities, customer histories, tool integrations) so AI truly understands how your operations run. Unlike basic assistants that stop at suggestions, our enterprise AI agents research across your full tech stack, synthesize insights, and take action: creating documents, filing tickets, generating reports, and automating workflows. With enterprise-grade security, 25+ integrations (Slack, Jira, Salesforce, Google Drive, and more), and a 2-3 day setup, teams save 8-10 hours weekly while getting 3x the value at half the cost of alternatives. [...] #### How can stakeholder involvement improve using AI to enhance business operations?

Involve key stakeholders from various departments to ensure that goals reflect broader organizational priorities and to identify high-impact areas, such as supply chain streamlining or enhanced customer interactions.

#### Why should AI selection focus on measurable outcomes?

Choose specific, measurable outcomes when selecting AI solutions that create operational improvements. For example, an enterprise AI agent like Coworker addresses the limitation of current tools lacking deep knowledge of your organization. Coworker acts as an intelligent AI agent who understands company-wide priorities, projects, and relationships, handling complex work spanning multiple departments. [...] On this page

results.title: AI as Employee, Not Software: Transforming Project Management Teams Through Virtual Collaboration - Coworked
results.content:
Transforming Team Structures With AI Coworkers

Integrating AI as team members rather than tools necessitates rethinking traditional team structures and workflows. Research indicates we're moving toward a future where project management will involve hybrid collaborative teams, human project managers leading diverse AI agents with specialized capabilities.

Emerging Team Models

Several team models are emerging as organizations integrate AI employees: [...] Conclusion: Embracing AI as Collaborative Partners

The shift from viewing AI as software tools to recognizing them as virtual employees represents a transformative opportunity for PMOs. By embracing this paradigm shift, organizations can create powerful partnerships that leverage the unique strengths of both human and AI team members.

For PMOs looking to stay ahead of the curve, the time to rethink the relationship between human project managers and AI systems is now. At Coworked, our AI project manager Harmony exemplifies this approach—acting as an autonomous virtual project manager that initiates, plans, manages, and closes projects like human PMs, but with the advantage of 24/7 operation and complete historical context. [...] Unlike traditional approaches that limited AI to a supportive role, this model fosters balanced interaction, enabling AI to act as a responsive partner that evolves with users over time. As one project manager noted, "AI doesn't just follow instructions; it contributes insights I might have missed and learns from our interactions to become a more effective partner".

AI as Virtual Team Member: A New Collaborative Framework

When organizations conceptualize AI as a virtual employee rather than software, they establish different expectations and integration approaches. At Coworked, our AI project manager Harmony exemplifies this transition, functioning not as a tool but as an autonomous team member who initiates, plans, manages, and closes projects with complete historical context.

results.title: The 6 best AI project management tools in 2026
results.content:
This year, Asana is joining the AI teammate trend: currently in beta, you can create AI agents with custom instructions, connect them to your data, and have them execute tasks while updating your workspace. The building process is accessible to non-technical people via the AI Studio, with templates available if you're unsure how to start. This adds AI-driven automation, another opportunity to free up your precious time for more critical tasks. [...] Add new Google Calendar appointments to an Asana task list

Try it

 Google Calendar logo

Google Calendar, Asana

Google Calendar + Asana

Asana price: AI included in paid plans starting at $13.49/user/month

## Best AI project manager for asking questions about your projects

### ClickUp (Web, macOS, Windows, iOS, Android)

ClickUp pros:

 Great price considering feature set
 Robust collaboration tools

ClickUp cons:

 Minor bugs from time to time

ClickUp is an all-in-one app, offering dashboards, chat channels, and project management. It offers both the ClickUp Brain for an AI chat-driven experience to control your workspace, and AI agents that can double as machine teammates that get stuff done for you. [...] While everyone on your team is keeping an eye on the calendar, Motion's AI is keeping an eye on task data. Based on team capacity and active work, it can predict when a project will end and alert you if it starts veering off-track. You can have a real-time view of progress as it happens, so you don't have to constantly check in with people. To simplify project planning, you can generate plans with the AI Projects feature to get tasks, assignments, and due dates ready so you can start moving as soon as possible.

results.title: The 5 Best AI Project Management Tools (Tested & Ranked)
results.content:
But here's the part I find really cool—the AI-assisted goal setting and project creation.

Just tell Asana what you're trying to achieve, and it'll help you break it down into actionable steps and tasks.

It's like having a friendly strategic planning buddy by your side!

While Asana's AI tools are handy for generating ideas and content, they can't actually improve your processes or manage projects for you.

Think of the AI as a knowledgeable assistant that can help you brainstorm and organize your thoughts, but you're still the one calling the shots and doing the real work.

#### What I disliked

While Asana's AI features are solid, the tool itself is a bit of a jack of all trades, master of none. [...] And if you're looking to take your automation to the next level, Trello integrates seamlessly with Zapier, giving you access to a whole world of advanced automation possibilities.

#### What I disliked

While Trello is great for basic project management and task tracking, it can feel a bit limited if you're working on more complex projects.

It lacks some of the advanced features you'll find in tools like Motion, Asana or ClickUp, like Gantt charts or resource management.

It's also worth noting that the AI features are currently only available in the Premium and Enterprise plans, which can get pricey for larger teams.

Read more: The best Trello alternatives

## Best for teams that want integrated AI writing and Q&A features
